tool.name: Squidpy Image
tool.description: perform image processing and feature extraction on spatial imaging data
tool.command:
NUMBA_NUM_THREADS=\$GALAXY_SLOTS && mkdir -p input output && unzip -q '$input_spatialdata' -d input &&
## Rename directory to spatialdata if it has a different name dir_name=\$(ls -d input/*/ | head -1 | xargs basename) && if [ "\$dir_name" != "spatialdata" ]; then mv "input/\$dir_name" input/spatialdata; fi &&
## run the image operation cat 'squidpy_image.py' && python3 'squidpy_image.py' && cd output && zip -r ../spatialdata.spatialdata.zip spatialdata/ && cd ..

configfiles.configfile:
import spatialdata as sd import squidpy as sq import numpy as np from spatialdata.models import Image2DModel, Image3DModel
## Load the SpatialData object sdata = sd.read_zarr("input/spatialdata")
print("Input SpatialData object:") print(sdata)
#if $operation_condi.operation == 'process': ## Process image print("\nProcessing image...") from squidpy.im import ImageContainer import xarray as xr
## Get the image from SpatialData #if $operation_condi.image_key: img_key = '$operation_condi.image_key' #else: ## Auto-detect first image img_key = list(sdata.images.keys())[0] print(f"Auto-detected image key: {img_key}") #end if
## Extract the actual image data from SpatialData ## SpatialData images are DataTree objects, need to get the underlying array img_data = sdata.images[img_key] if hasattr(img_data, 'to_dataset'): ## DataTree object - convert to DataArray img_array = img_data['scale0']['image'].data else: ## Already an array-like object img_array = img_data
img = ImageContainer(img_array)
img_processed = sq.im.process( img, #if $operation_condi.layer: layer = '$operation_condi.layer', #else: layer = None, #end if #if $operation_condi.library_id: library_id = '$operation_condi.library_id', #else: library_id = None, #end if method='$operation_condi.method_condi.method', #if $operation_condi.chunks: #if str($operation_condi.chunks) == '-1': chunks = 'auto', #else: chunks = $operation_condi.chunks, #end if #else: chunks = None, #end if lazy=$operation_condi.lazy_bool, #if $operation_condi.layer_added: layer_added = '$operation_condi.layer_added', #else: layer_added = None, #end if #if $operation_condi.channel_dim: channel_dim = '$operation_condi.channel_dim', #else: channel_dim = None, #end if copy=True, #if str($operation_condi.method_condi.method) =="smooth" and $operation_condi.method_condi.sigma: sigma = $operation_condi.method_condi.sigma #else if str($operation_condi.method_condi.method) =="smooth" and str($operation_condi.method_condi.sigma) == "": sigma = 1 #end if )
print("\nProcessed image:") print(img_processed)
## Store back in SpatialData #if $operation_condi.output_image_key: output_key = '$operation_condi.output_image_key' #else: output_key = f"{img_key}_processed" #end if
## Get the processed layer name #if $operation_condi.layer_added: processed_layer = '$operation_condi.layer_added' #else: processed_layer = list(img_processed.data.keys())[-1] #end if
## Rename 'channels' dimension to 'c' for SpatialData compatibility processed_img = img_processed[processed_layer] ## Handle various channel dimension names (channels, channels:0, etc.) channel_dim = None for dim in processed_img.dims: if dim.startswith('channels'): channel_dim = dim break if channel_dim: processed_img = processed_img.rename({channel_dim: 'c'})
## Parse with appropriate SpatialData model if 'z' in processed_img.dims: processed_img = Image3DModel.parse(processed_img) else: processed_img = Image2DModel.parse(processed_img)
sdata.images[output_key] = processed_img
#else if $operation_condi.operation == 'segment': ## Segment image print("\nSegmenting image...") from squidpy.im import ImageContainer import xarray as xr
## Get the image from SpatialData #if $operation_condi.image_key: img_key = '$operation_condi.image_key' #else: ## Auto-detect first image img_key = list(sdata.images.keys())[0] print(f"Auto-detected image key: {img_key}") #end if
## Extract the actual image data from SpatialData ## SpatialData images are DataTree objects, need to get the underlying array img_data = sdata.images[img_key] if hasattr(img_data, 'to_dataset'): ## DataTree object - convert to DataArray img_array = img_data['scale0']['image'].data else: ## Already an array-like object img_array = img_data
img = ImageContainer(img_array)
## Watershed segmentation img_segmented = sq.im.segment( img, #if $operation_condi.layer: layer = '$operation_condi.layer', #else: layer = None, #end if #if $operation_condi.library_id: library_id = '$operation_condi.library_id', #else: library_id = None, #end if method='watershed', channel = $operation_condi.channel, #if $operation_condi.chunks: #if str($operation_condi.chunks) == '-1': chunks = 'auto', #else: chunks = $operation_condi.chunks, #end if #else: chunks = None, #end if lazy=$operation_condi.lazy_bool, #if $operation_condi.layer_added: layer_added = '$operation_condi.layer_added', #else: layer_added = None, #end if copy=True )
print("\nSegmented image:") print(img_segmented)
## Store back in SpatialData #if $operation_condi.output_image_key: output_key = '$operation_condi.output_image_key' #else: output_key = f"{img_key}_segmented" #end if
## Get the segmented layer name #if $operation_condi.layer_added: segmented_layer = '$operation_condi.layer_added' #else: segmented_layer = list(img_segmented.data.keys())[-1] #end if
## Rename 'channels' dimension to 'c' for SpatialData compatibility segmented_img = img_segmented[segmented_layer] ## Handle various channel dimension names (channels, channels:0, etc.) channel_dim = None for dim in segmented_img.dims: if dim.startswith('channels'): channel_dim = dim break if channel_dim: segmented_img = segmented_img.rename({channel_dim: 'c'})
## Parse with appropriate SpatialData model if 'z' in segmented_img.dims: segmented_img = Image3DModel.parse(segmented_img) else: segmented_img = Image2DModel.parse(segmented_img)
sdata.images[output_key] = segmented_img
#else if $operation_condi.operation == 'calculate_image_features': ## Calculate image features print("\nCalculating image features...") from squidpy.im import ImageContainer import xarray as xr import os
## Get the image from SpatialData #if $operation_condi.image_key: img_key = '$operation_condi.image_key' #else: ## Auto-detect first image img_key = list(sdata.images.keys())[0] print(f"Auto-detected image key: {img_key}") #end if
## Extract the actual image data from SpatialData ## SpatialData images are DataTree objects, need to get the underlying array img_data = sdata.images[img_key] if hasattr(img_data, 'to_dataset'): ## DataTree object - convert to DataArray img_array = img_data['scale0']['image'].data else: ## Already an array-like object img_array = img_data
img = ImageContainer(img_array)
## Get the AnnData table #if $operation_condi.table_key: table_key = '$operation_condi.table_key' #else: table_key = 'table' #end if
adata = sdata.tables[table_key]
print(f"Using table: {table_key}") print(f"AnnData shape: {adata.shape}")
## Build features_kwargs features_kwargs = {}
## Texture feature kwargs #if 'texture' in $operation_condi.features: features_kwargs['texture'] = {} #if $operation_condi.texture_props: #set props_list = [str(x.strip()) for x in str($operation_condi.texture_props).split(',')] features_kwargs['texture']['props'] = tuple($props_list) #end if #if $operation_condi.texture_channels: #set channels_list = [int(x.strip()) for x in str($operation_condi.texture_channels).split(',')] features_kwargs['texture']['channels'] = $channels_list #end if #if $operation_condi.texture_distances: #set distances_list = [int(x.strip()) for x in str($operation_condi.texture_distances).split(',')] features_kwargs['texture']['distances'] = tuple($distances_list) #end if #if $operation_condi.texture_angles: #set angles_list = [float(x.strip()) for x in str($operation_condi.texture_angles).split(',')] features_kwargs['texture']['angles'] = tuple($angles_list) #end if #end if
## Summary feature kwargs #if 'summary' in $operation_condi.features: features_kwargs['summary'] = {} #if $operation_condi.summary_quantiles: #set quantiles_list = [float(x.strip()) for x in str($operation_condi.summary_quantiles).split(',')] features_kwargs['summary']['quantiles'] = tuple($quantiles_list) #end if #if $operation_condi.summary_channels: #set channels_list = [int(x.strip()) for x in str($operation_condi.summary_channels).split(',')] features_kwargs['summary']['channels'] = $channels_list #end if #end if
## Histogram feature kwargs #if 'histogram' in $operation_condi.features: features_kwargs['histogram'] = {} #if $operation_condi.histogram_bins: features_kwargs['histogram']['bins'] = $operation_condi.histogram_bins #end if #if $operation_condi.histogram_channels: #set channels_list = [int(x.strip()) for x in str($operation_condi.histogram_channels).split(',')] features_kwargs['histogram']['channels'] = $channels_list #end if #if $operation_condi.histogram_v_range: #set v_range_list = [int(x.strip()) for x in str($operation_condi.histogram_v_range).split(',')] features_kwargs['histogram']['v_range'] = tuple($v_range_list) #end if #end if
## Segmentation feature kwargs #if 'segmentation' in $operation_condi.features: features_kwargs['segmentation'] = {} #if $operation_condi.segmentation_label_layer: features_kwargs['segmentation']['label_layer'] = '$operation_condi.segmentation_label_layer' #end if #if $operation_condi.segmentation_intensity_layer: features_kwargs['segmentation']['intensity_layer'] = '$operation_condi.segmentation_intensity_layer' #end if #if $operation_condi.segmentation_props: #set props_list = [str(x.strip()) for x in str($operation_condi.segmentation_props).split(',')] features_kwargs['segmentation']['props'] = tuple($props_list) #end if #if $operation_condi.segmentation_channels: #set channels_list = [int(x.strip()) for x in str($operation_condi.segmentation_channels).split(',')] features_kwargs['segmentation']['channels'] = $channels_list #end if #end if
sq.im.calculate_image_features( adata, img, #if $operation_condi.layer: layer = '$operation_condi.layer', #else: layer = None, #end if #if $operation_condi.library_id: library_id = '$operation_condi.library_id', #else: library_id = None, #end if #if $operation_condi.features: #set features_list = [str(x.strip()) for x in str($operation_condi.features).split(',')] features = $features_list, #else: features = 'summary', #end if features_kwargs=features_kwargs, #if $operation_condi.key_added: key_added = '$operation_condi.key_added', #else: key_added = 'img_features', #end if copy=False, n_jobs=int(os.getenv("GALAXY_SLOTS", 4)), backend='loky', show_progress_bar=False )
print(f"\nImage features calculated and stored in adata.obsm['{key_added}']")
## Update the table in SpatialData sdata.tables[table_key] = adata
#end if
print("\nOperation completed successfully!") print("\nUpdated SpatialData object:") print(sdata)
## Save the result sdata.write("./output/spatialdata", overwrite=True)

param.name: sigma
param.label: Sigma
param.help: Standard deviation for Gaussian kernel

param.name: chunks
param.label: Chunks
param.help: Number of chunks for Dask. For automatic chunking, use -1

param.name: lazy_bool
param.label: Lazy computation?
param.help: Whether to lazily compute the result. Only used when chunks is specified

param.name: layer_added
param.label: Output layer name
param.help: Name of the new image layer. If not provided, it uses '{layer}_{method}'

param.name: channel_dim
param.label: Channel dimension name
param.help: Name of the channel dimension. Default is same as original if processing doesn't change channel count

param.name: output_image_key
param.label: Output image key
param.help: Key to store processed image in SpatialData. If not provided, uses '{image_key}_processed'

param.name: channel
param.label: Channel
param.help: Channel index to use for segmentation.

param.name: chunks
param.label: Chunks
param.help: Number of chunks for Dask. For automatic chunking, use -1

param.name: lazy_bool
param.label: Lazy computation?
param.help: Whether to lazily compute the result. Only used when chunks is specified

param.name: layer_added
param.label: Output layer name
param.help: Name of the new segmented layer. If not provided, uses 'segmented_{method}'

param.name: output_image_key
param.label: Output image key
param.help: Key to store segmented image in SpatialData. If not provided, uses '{image_key}_segmented'

param.name: table_key
param.label: Table key
param.help: Key in SpatialData.tables where the AnnData table is stored

param.name: features
param.label: Features to calculate
param.help: Select one or more feature types

param.name: texture_props
param.label: Texture properties
param.help: Haralick texture properties to compute (only used if 'texture' is selected). Default: contrast, dissimilarity, homogeneity, correlation, ASM

param.name: texture_channels
param.label: Texture channels
param.help: Comma-separated channel indices for texture calculation, e.g., '0,1,2'. If empty, uses all channels (only used if 'texture' is selected)

param.name: texture_distances
param.label: Texture distances
param.help: Comma-separated distances for GLCM, e.g., '1,2,3'. Default: 1 (only used if 'texture' is selected)

param.name: texture_angles
param.label: Texture angles
param.help: Comma-separated angles in radians for GLCM. Default: 0, π/4, π/2, 3π/4 (only used if 'texture' is selected)

param.name: summary_quantiles
param.label: Summary quantiles
param.help: Comma-separated quantiles to compute, e.g., '0.9,0.5,0.1'. Default: 0.9, 0.5, 0.1 (only used if 'summary' is selected)

param.name: summary_channels
param.label: Summary channels
param.help: Comma-separated channel indices for summary statistics, e.g., '0,1,2'. If empty, uses all channels (only used if 'summary' is selected)

param.name: histogram_bins
param.label: Histogram bins
param.help: Number of bins for histogram. Default: 10 (only used if 'histogram' is selected)

param.name: histogram_channels
param.label: Histogram channels
param.help: Comma-separated channel indices for histogram, e.g., '0,1,2'. If empty, uses all channels (only used if 'histogram' is selected)

param.name: histogram_v_range
param.label: Histogram value range
param.help: Two comma-separated values defining range, e.g., '0,255'. If empty, uses whole image range (only used if 'histogram' is selected)

param.name: segmentation_label_layer
param.label: Segmentation label layer
param.help: Name of the image layer containing segmentation labels (only used if 'segmentation' is selected)

param.name: segmentation_intensity_layer
param.label: Segmentation intensity layer
param.help: Name of the image layer for intensity properties. (only used if 'segmentation' is selected)

param.name: segmentation_props
param.label: Segmentation properties
param.help: Properties to compute for each segment (only used if 'segmentation' is selected). Default: label, area, mean_intensity
param.option: Area - number of pixels of segment
mean intensity of intensity_layer in segment

param.name: segmentation_channels
param.label: Segmentation channels
param.help: Comma-separated channel indices for intensity properties, e.g., '0,1,2'. If empty, uses all channels (only used if 'segmentation' is selected and intensity_layer is provided)

param.name: key_added
param.label: Key for results
param.help: Key in adata.obsm where calculated features will be stored

tool.help:
**What it does**
This tool performs image processing and feature extraction on spatial imaging data using Squidpy.
**Operations**
**Image Processing:**
- **Process Image**: Apply transformations to high-resolution images:
- **Smooth**: Apply Gaussian filtering to reduce noise (uses skimage.filters.gaussian) - **Grayscale**: Convert RGB images to grayscale (uses skimage.color.rgb2gray)
**Image Segmentation:**
- **Segment Image**: Segment images to identify cellular or tissue structures:
- **Watershed**: Watershed segmentation algorithm (uses skimage.segmentation.watershed) - Processes specified channel (default: channel 0) - Can optionally use Dask for large images
**Feature Extraction:**
- **Calculate Image Features**: Extract quantitative features from images for each observation in the dataset:
- **Summary**: Basic statistics (mean, std, min, max, and configurable quantiles) for each channel - **Texture**: Haralick texture features from gray level co-occurrence matrices (GLCM) - Configurable properties: contrast, dissimilarity, homogeneity, correlation, ASM, energy - Customizable distances and angles for GLCM computation - Channel selection support - **Histogram**: Distribution of pixel intensities across configurable number of bins - Optional channel selection and value range specification - **Segmentation**: Statistics computed from segmentation masks (requires a label layer) - Properties include area, centroid, intensities, shape descriptors - Supports both label-based and intensity-based properties - Optional intensity layer for calculating intensity statistics
**Input**
- A SpatialData object (.zip format) containing spatial imaging data - Operation-specific parameters: - Image key: which image in the SpatialData object to process - Layer: specific layer within the image (optional) - Library ID: Z-dimension(s) to process (optional) - Method-specific parameters
**Output**
- Modified SpatialData (.zip) object with: - **Process/Segment**: New processed/segmented image layers added to the images - **Calculate Features**: Features stored in the AnnData table at `adata.obsm['{key_added}']`
**Notes**
- Image processing operations create new image layers without modifying originals - Segmentation requires appropriate preprocessing (e.g., smoothing) for best results - Feature calculation links image data to observations in the AnnData table - Dask chunking (`chunks` parameter) enables processing of large images that don't fit in memory - Lazy computation (`lazy=True`) defers computation until results are needed - For multi-Z-stack images, `library_id` controls which Z-dimensions are processed
**Performance Tips**
- Use `chunks='auto'` for automatic memory management with large images - Set `lazy=True` when chaining multiple operations - Adjust `sigma` in smoothing based on image resolution and noise level - Feature calculation parallelizes across observations using available CPU cores
-----
**More Information**
- `Squidpy documentation <https://squidpy.readthedocs.io/>`__ - `Squidpy image API <https://squidpy.readthedocs.io/en/stable/api.html#module-squidpy.im>`__
